GTM Phase 2
Ovie · Brand Identity · GTM Phase 2

Brand Direction for Ovie.

Both identities serve the same product and the same user. What differs is the emotional register — the relationship Ovie chooses to have with the people who use it. Walk through both directions here so the differences are felt, not just read.

Sandy Warmth
vs
Pure White & Cobalt
01

Brand personality

Sandy Warmth resolves anxiety through warmth — "we're here with you."
Pure White & Cobalt resolves anxiety through authority — "we know what we're doing."
Identity A
Sandy Warmth
The Trusted Guide
"We're here to help you understand your coverage. Don't worry — we'll explain everything clearly and simply."
Personality adjectives
WarmApproachable ReassuringEditorial EmpatheticHuman CalmCaring FriendlyInviting
Identity B
Pure White & Cobalt
Calm Authority
"We know exactly what we're doing. Here is the analysis — precise, cited, and actionable."
Personality adjectives
PreciseConfident ExpertStructured AuthoritativeEfficient TrustworthyCalm DirectData-led
02

Colour palette

Sandy Warmth uses warm earth tones — teal, terracotta, amber. Colours that feel organic and human. Every palette moment says "a person made this."
Pure White & Cobalt uses institutional cool tones — cobalt, crimson, saffron. Colours associated with financial systems and precision tools. Every palette moment says "this is a credible system."
Sandy Warmth — why each colour was chosen
Teal — Primary #0C8CA6
Tint #DBEEF2
Sits in the blue-green spectrum — carries both trust and nature. Where cobalt feels institutional, teal feels like it is on your side. Signals warmth and protection.
Terracotta — High severity #B55512
Tint #F7DDD1
Signals urgency without alarm. Unlike red, terracotta reads as important, worth reviewing — not dangerous. The warm tint keeps the user calm while being informed.
Amber — Medium severity #9A7010
Tint #F5ECD8
Signals caution without alarm. The warm tint feels like a handwritten note in the margin — gentle awareness rather than a formal warning.
Emerald — Confirmed / covered #1A8C4E
Tint #DFF5EB
The reassurance colour. Used when Ovie confirms coverage. Says "you are safe here" — not the clinical pass/fail of a red/green system.
Pure White & Cobalt — why each colour was chosen
Cobalt — Primary #2056D4
Tint #E8F0FB
Pure blue — no green warmth. The colour of Revolut, Linear, Stripe. Where teal feels approachable, cobalt feels credible and authoritative. Used sparingly, every cobalt moment carries institutional weight.
Crimson — High severity #C0392B
Tint #FAEAEA
Cooler and more clinical than terracotta. Reads as a system flag — factual and precise. Authoritative rather than alarming. A data point, not a siren.
Saffron — Medium severity #C47D10
Tint #FDF3E0
A cooler, more saturated amber. On a white canvas it reads as a structured system notification — this data point is flagged, not "you should be concerned."
Emerald — Confirmed / covered #1A7A4A
Tint #E2F5EC
Slightly cooler than Sandy Warmth's emerald. Signals confirmed and protected — factual, not warm. Creates the covered/gap axis the insights screen is built on.
Sandy Warmth canvas — #FAF8F4
Warm white. Signals organic, editorial, considered before content loads. Reduces visual anxiety before the user reads a word.
Pure White canvas — #F6F7F9
Cool white. Everything on it reads as deliberate and structured. 2 degrees cooler — a completely different emotional ground.
03

Typography

Sandy Warmth uses Source Serif Pro for all headings. The organic serif letterforms signal human authorship — a person curated this, not a system.
Pure White & Cobalt uses DM Sans throughout — no serif anywhere. 700 Bold headings replace serif warmth with structural authority.
Sandy Warmth
Display / screen title
Car Insurance
Source Serif Pro · 26px · Weight 600
Card heading
Your Policies
Source Serif Pro · 22px · Weight 600
Section heading
Ovie Insights
Source Serif Pro · 18px · Weight 600
Body
Your policy excludes flooding. Given your zip code this is worth reviewing before next season.
Source Sans 3 · 15px · Weight 400
Label / tag
State Farm · Active
Source Sans 3 · 13px · Weight 500
Caption / metadata
Expires 31 Sep 2026 · 1 Year term
Source Sans 3 · 12px · Weight 400
Source Serif Pro at heading level carries warmth through its organic letterforms before a word is read. "Car Insurance" in serif feels like a thoughtful guide wrote it. The same words in 700 bold sans feels like a system generated it. That gap is the entire personality difference.
Pure White & Cobalt
Display / screen title
Car Insurance
DM Sans · 24px · Weight 700
Card heading
Your Policies
DM Sans · 20px · Weight 700
Section heading
Ovie Insights
DM Sans · 17px · Weight 700
Body
Your policy excludes flooding. Given your zip code this is worth reviewing before next season.
DM Sans · 15px · Weight 400
Label / tag
State Farm · Active
DM Sans · 12px · Weight 700 · 0.06em tracking · uppercase
Caption / metadata
Expires 31 Sep 2026 · 1 Year term
DM Sans · 12px · Weight 400 · #4A5568 slate
No serif appears anywhere — not even once. The 700 Bold weight replaces the warmth the serif provided with structural authority. Labels use 0.06em tracking at 700 weight — the fintech label treatment used by Revolut and Linear. That single detail signals precision system over friendly app.
04

Icon usage

Sandy Warmth — line icons inside warm teal containers. The container cushions the icon. Every tile is a personality moment, not just a functional marker.
Pure White & Cobalt — filled icons with no container. The icon sits directly on white. Functional signal, no decorative cushioning.
Sandy Warmth
Line icons in teal containers
Each icon sits inside a rounded teal container with a warm background tint (#DBEEF2). The container cushions the icon and adds a warm personality moment to every tile. The icon feels invited into the design, not placed in it.
Container rule
Rounded square container · background #DBEEF2 · stroke #0C8CA6 · 1.5px line weight · 44×44px minimum touch target
Emotional effect
The container adds a teal moment to every tile it occupies — personality beyond function. The document grid feels like a personal document wallet, not a data index.
Pure White & Cobalt
Filled icons · no container
Icons are filled cobalt (#2056D4), sitting directly on the white card surface with no container. No visual cushioning — the icon is a functional data point, not a decorative element. The white space around it communicates confidence.
Placement rule
No container · filled cobalt #2056D4 · sits directly on white card surface · 20–24px icon size · 44×44px minimum touch target
Emotional effect
Without a container, the card surface breathes. The icon is a data point, not a decorative tile. More whitespace communicates confidence — nothing is decorated unnecessarily.
05

Voice & tone

Sandy Warmth speaks like a trusted friend — warm, direct, personal. Uses "you" and "we." Leads with empathy before data.
Pure White & Cobalt speaks like a confident expert — precise, declarative, data-first. No filler words. Leads with the number before the context.
Sandy Warmth speaks like a trusted friend
Upload complete
"Great job! Your Car Insurance is ready. We found 3 things worth reviewing — let's take a look together."
Coverage gap found
"Your auto liability coverage may leave you exposed to a gap of around $450,000. This is worth addressing before your renewal."
Renewal reminder
"Just a heads up — your State Farm policy renews in 83 days. Renewing early could save you up to 12%."
Write this
Warm, directEmpatheticAction-orientedPlain languageReassuring
Not this
Insurance jargonAlarmingOverly formalCorporate
Pure White & Cobalt speaks like a confident expert
Upload complete
"Analysis complete. 3 watchpoints identified across your Car Insurance policy. Review below."
Coverage gap found
"Auto liability limit ($100,000) creates an estimated $450,000 exposure gap against median personal injury claims in your state."
Renewal reminder
"State Farm policy expires in 83 days. Early renewal typically reduces premium 8–12%. Review renewal options."
Write this
PreciseData-ledDeclarativeSpecific numbersNo filler words
Not this
Exclamation marksOverly casualVague estimatesHedging
06

Screens

Sandy Warmth — teal fills, serif headings, warm alert borders, icon containers. Every element adds warmth to the screen.
Pure White & Cobalt — white cards, bold sans headings, top border alerts, bare filled icons. Every element communicates precision.
